description: Skill registry for extensible agent capabilities. Power users define custom prompts or flow references.

columns.description: Primary column — unique human-readable name for the skill (e.g., 'Summarize Thread', 'Extract Action Items').
Natural language description of what the skill does. Used by the agent for skill selection during triage.
Whether the skill is a prompt template executed inline or a reference to an external Power Automate flow.
Prompt text with {{PARAM_NAME}} substitution placeholders. Required when cr_skilltype = PROMPT_TEMPLATE. The agent resolves placeholders at runtime from the parameter schema.
JSON Schema defining input parameters for the skill. The agent validates inputs against this schema before execution.
Power Automate flow GUID or HTTP trigger URL. Required when cr_skilltype = FLOW_REFERENCE. Nullable for PROMPT_TEMPLATE skills.
How the skill output should be rendered in the UI.
Toggle to enable or disable this skill. Disabled skills are not available for agent selection.
Whether this skill is admin-shared and visible to all users. When true, the skill appears in every user's skill list regardless of ownership.
Timestamp of the most recent skill invocation. Nullable — null means the skill has never been used. Updated by the agent flow after execution.
Total number of times this skill has been invoked. Incremented by the agent flow after each execution.

notes.design_rationale: UserOwned so each power user manages their own skills. Admin-shared skills (cr_isshared = true) are queried separately and merged into every user's available skill list. The agent selects skills based on cr_skilldescription during triage — the description must be clear enough for semantic matching.
notes.prompt_template_format: Prompt templates use {{PARAM_NAME}} double-brace substitution (not Liquid or Handlebars). The agent resolves parameters from the input signal context and the cr_parameterschema definition. Unresolved placeholders are left as-is — the agent handles missing parameters gracefully.
notes.flow_reference_pattern: For FLOW_REFERENCE skills, the agent calls the flow via HTTP trigger URL and passes the resolved parameter JSON as the request body. The flow must return a JSON response matching the expected output format.
notes.security: Table uses UserOwned ownership type. Row-level security is enforced via an ownership-based security role so each user's custom skills are private. Admin-shared skills bypass row-level security via a Dataverse view scoped to cr_isshared = true.
notes.system_columns: Standard Dataverse system columns (ownerid, createdon, modifiedon, statecode, statuscode) are created automatically and do not need to be defined here.
